# Architecture Review Manual

Purpose: define a repeatable architecture-review process that produces concrete, decision-ready change stories.

## Operating cadence

- Run a review at least every two weeks.
- Run an additional review before larger releases or structural migrations.
- Review scope is delta-based: changed backend/frontend/data/spec flows since the previous review.

## Review output contract

Each cycle outputs:

- prioritized findings (risk-oriented)
- concrete architecture change stories
- estimated effort (`S`, `M`, `L`)
- migration and compatibility notes

## Decision protocol

- User decides each story with one status:
  - `accept`
  - `defer`
  - `reject`
- Only `accept` stories proceed to:
  1. architecture artifact updates (rules/spec/manual),
  2. implementation,
  3. verification.

## Required architecture artifacts on accepted stories

- Rules in `.cursor/rules/` when governance/guardrails change.
- Requirements in `spec/requirements/` when domain or architecture intent changes.
- Operational docs in `doc/` when workflows/commands/operator behavior change.

Canonical requirement artifacts for this process:

- `spec/requirements/GEN-Architektur-Spezifikation.md` (baseline architecture intent)
- `spec/requirements/GEN-Architektur-Backlog.md` (review cycle stories and decisions)
- `spec/requirements/GEN-Spec-Spezifikation.md` (spec-system governance conventions)

Canonical supporting architecture artifacts:

- `doc/adr/ADR-TEMPLATE.qmd` and `doc/adr/ADR-*.qmd` (architecture decision records)
- `doc/architecture-glossary.qmd` (shared process/state/data terms)
- `spec/domain/gen-domain.puml` and `spec/domain/gen-paths-domain.puml` (high-level structure and path visualization)

## Expanded trigger set (concept-level + structural)

Use the review not only for structural drift but also for concept-fit checks:

- process modeling fit:
  - if multi-step business flows become complex or stateful, evaluate explicit process/case modeling.
- medical data semantics fit:
  - evaluate whether observation semantics and unit handling should follow standards.
- state/event fit:
  - evaluate whether implicit transitions should become explicit state/event models.
- automation fit:
  - evaluate whether repeated architecture rules should become CI fitness checks.

## External reference scan (when needed)

For concept-fit questions, include short benchmark checks against established standards/frameworks:

- LOINC: what is measured (observation semantics)
  - [https://loinc.org/about](https://loinc.org/about)
- UCUM: how units are encoded and exchanged
  - [https://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum](https://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum)
- BPM+/workflow references for healthcare process modeling (BPMN/CMMN/DMN context)
  - [https://www.bpm-plus.org/healthcare-and-bpmn.htm](https://www.bpm-plus.org/healthcare-and-bpmn.htm)
- Architecture fitness functions for continuous governance
  - [https://continuous-architecture.org/docs/practices/fitness-functions.html](https://continuous-architecture.org/docs/practices/fitness-functions.html)

## Suggested story template

- Story ID and title
- Why (current drift/risk)
- Target architecture state
- Scope (files/modules/interfaces)
- Acceptance criteria
- Migration/backfill notes

## Completion note for each accepted story

- What drift was reduced
- What was intentionally not refactored (and why)
- Recommended follow-up decomposition
